channel.title: AI Daily Update
channel.description: Daily high-signal AI intelligence digest -- The Big Three, new tools, hardware and enterprise adoption.

item.title: Claude "Mythos" Leak Reveals Anthropic's Most Powerful and Riskiest Model Yet
item.description: A leaked Anthropic draft blog post unveiled a new model called Claude Mythos, described internally as "a step change" in AI performance and the company's most capable build to date. The leaked documents warn Mythos poses unprecedented cybersecurity risks, potentially enabling rapid automated discovery and exploitation of software vulnerabilities. The model is currently being trialed by select early access customers ahead of an official launch announcement.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: NVIDIA Unveils Rubin Platform at GTC 2026, Jensen Huang Eyes $1 Trillion in AI Chip Revenue
item.description: At GTC 2026, NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang introduced the Vera Rubin next-generation AI platform combining six new chips, a Groq 3 LPU inference processor, and a rack-scale AI supercomputer. The Rubin NVL72 systems deliver 35x higher throughput per megawatt than prior-gen hardware. Huang projected NVIDIA's AI chip portfolio will generate over $1 trillion in cumulative revenue through 2027, cementing the company's commanding position in AI infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: White House Releases National AI Legislative Framework, Targeting State-Level Preemption
item.description: The Trump administration published its National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence on March 20, laying out legislative recommendations that would preempt state AI regulations deemed burdensome to innovation. The framework proposes no new federal AI regulator, directs existing sector agencies, and introduces child safety mandates, data center permitting standards, and energy use guidelines. It is widely seen as a direct challenge to state AI bills already enacted in Colorado, Texas, and California.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: Tool Spotlight: Cursor AI Launches Parallel Subagents and BugBot for Automated PR Reviews
item.description: Cursor's March 2026 release introduces two headline features: parallel subagents that allow the AI coding assistant to execute discrete subtasks concurrently within a single session, dramatically accelerating complex refactors and multi-file projects; and BugBot, an autonomous code review agent that runs on every pull request, proactively flagging regressions, logic errors, and style violations before a human reviews the diff. Why it matters: parallel execution fundamentally changes the economics of AI-assisted development, pushing coding tools from assistant to genuine autonomous co-developer territory.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: OpenAI GPT-5.4 Arrives with 1M-Token Context, Tool Search API and Native Computer Use
item.description: Released March 5, GPT-5.4 brings a 1-million-token context window, a revamped Tool Search system for multi-tool agents, and built-in computer use via the Responses API. Companion models GPT-5.4 mini and nano cover high-volume use cases at lower cost, while GPT-5.4 Thinking and GPT-5.4 Pro offer reasoning-optimized variants for professional work.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: Gemini 3.1 Pro Tops Reasoning Benchmarks with 77.1% ARC-AGI-2 Score
item.description: Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro launches as the highest-scoring general-purpose AI model on ARC-AGI-2 (77.1%) and GPQA Diamond (94.3% graduate-level science), outpacing both Claude and GPT-5. Critically, Google kept pricing identical to its predecessor, making it a straight upgrade at no extra cost for existing API customers.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: Google Gemini Adds ChatGPT Chat Import Tool to Attract Users from Rivals
item.description: Google rolled out a new import feature letting Gemini users upload zipped chat archives from ChatGPT and other AI providers, preserving conversation context when switching. The feature is available to both free and paid subscribers and is seen as a direct move to reduce friction for users considering migrating away from OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: Microsoft Unifies Copilot Under New EVP Jacob Andreou in Major Leadership Shake-Up
item.description: On March 17, Microsoft announced a sweeping reorganization of its Copilot strategy, promoting Jacob Andreou to Executive Vice President, Copilot, reporting directly to CEO Satya Nadella. Consumer and commercial Copilot efforts are now unified under one team, while Mustafa Suleyman's mandate shifts to frontier AI research with a five-year superintelligence horizon. Separately, Microsoft launched the Azure Copilot Migration Agent to automate cloud migration planning.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: Meta Plans Four New MTIA Chip Generations in Two Years to Reduce NVIDIA Reliance
item.description: Meta revealed an aggressive custom silicon roadmap, targeting four new generations of its MTIA (Meta Training and Inference Accelerator) chips within 24 months to cut reliance on NVIDIA for ranking, recommendation, and generative AI workloads. The announcement comes alongside news of Llama 4 Scout and Maverick, the company's first open natively multimodal mixture-of-experts models.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: OpenAI Raises Additional $10B, Pushing Record Funding Round to $120B Total
item.description: OpenAI secured another $10 billion in March, extending its already record-breaking $110 billion round from February to "north of $120 billion," per the company's CFO. The fresh capital accelerates data center build-out and compute investments as OpenAI competes with rivals Anthropic (recently valued at $380B) and xAI.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: EU Streamlines AI Act, Delays High-Risk AI Rules by Up to 16 Months
item.description: The EU Council agreed on March 13 to adjust the timeline for applying the AI Act's high-risk AI system rules by up to 16 months, contingent on the European Commission confirming that required standards and implementation tools are in place. The amendment is part of the "Omnibus VII" simplification package and reflects industry pressure to avoid compliance burdens before the regulatory toolkit is complete.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: Ex-DeepMind Robotics Startup Physical Intelligence in Talks for $11B Valuation
item.description: Physical Intelligence, founded two years ago by former Google DeepMind researchers and AI academics, is in advanced discussions for a new approximately $1 billion funding round at an $11 billion valuation. The company is building general-purpose foundation models for physical robots and has attracted significant enterprise interest from manufacturing and logistics sectors.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update
